Critical adsorption point (CAP) of PEO block
conditions.evidence_text: Reversed phase LC at CAP of PEO allowed for oligomeric separation of triblock copolymers with regard to PPO block... Solvent A: Acetone/Water = 37/63 v/v, Solvent B: Methanol/Water = 87/13 v/v... column oven was maintained at 50°C.
conditions.notes: Gradient elution used to separate PPO oligomers while PEO is at critical conditions.

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical adsorption point (CAP) of PPO block
conditions.evidence_text: Normal phase LC at CAP of PPO renders oligomeric separation with respect to PEO block... Isocratic separations at CAP of PPO were performed with mobile phase composition of acetonitrile/water = 80/20 v/v at a flow rate of 1.0 mL/min at 30°C.
conditions.notes: Used for 1D separation of PEO oligomers. Also used as first dimension in 2D-LC with slightly different ratio (84/16) and flow rate (0.05 mL/min).

conditions.detector: polymer laboratories PL ELS 2100, an evaporating light scattering detector
conditions.evidence_text: Reversed phase (RP) LC separates with regard to PO units of the copolymers at the critical conditions of PEO. On the contrary, Normal phase (NP) LC separates with respect to EO units of the copolymers with minimal effect of PPO block at its critical conditions.

conditions.detector: polymer laboratories PL ELS 2100, an evaporating light scattering detector
conditions.evidence_text: On the contrary, NPLC can be used to achieve CAP of PPO and interaction of PEO. In this study, we realized critical conditions of PPO on Nucleosil columns in acetonitrile-water mobile phase. PPOs show molar mass independent elution in the range of interest for this study (up to 2500 g/mol) in acetonitrile/water (80/20 v/v).
